pi_items.source: setup_task.sh explicitly creates all five of these users using CREATE USER statements.
export_result.sh checks for this exact policy name in the audit_unified_policies table.
export_result.sh explicitly checks for the existence and contents of this file path.
setup_task.sh and export_result.sh both use the 'system' username to authenticate queries against the database.
setup_task.sh explicitly uses 'OraclePassword123' as the password when connecting to the database as the system user.

privileged_info_summary: Completed PI audit for oracle_sql_developer_env security_privilege_audit task by confirming that the target users (DEV_USER, REPORT_USER2, ANALYST_USER, APP_USER, LEGACY_USER) and their excessive privileges are synthetically generated and explicitly injected via setup_task.sh. Verified that the expected unified audit policy name 'PRIVILEGE_ESCALATION_AUDIT', output report path '/home/ga/Documents/exports/security_remediation.txt', and system database credentials all exactly match the task description instructions and export script's database queries. Discovered that the remediation of 'ANALYST_USER', while requested in the prompt, is completely absent from the scoring logic in verifier.py.
